item.title: Director 1.1.3
item.description:
StageGlass Director — 1.1.3

The Desktop Wall arrives: Director becomes a fleet remote for free-roam desktops, plus a scene-export fix that shrinks packages by an order of magnitude.

  • Desktop Wall. A new tab in Remote mode controls every connected Desktop Player: live seat thumbnails, shuffle-the-wall, save the arrangement as a named scene, and reset every seat to its exact saved state between takes. Send a .sgfreeroam package to selected seats and it loads and opens automatically.
  • Per-seat operator controls. Blackout, screensaver, keyboard/mouse input lock, master brightness, and full color grading with temperature, contrast, saturation, and hue.
  • Players tab, rebuilt for mixed rigs. Hide background seats (or all free-roam desktops at once), pin your hero device for a full-size cue list while others stay compact, and scripted devices now read as primary at a glance.
  • Scene export slimmed dramatically. Exports no longer bundle entire character social libraries — scenes that shipped at hundreds of megabytes now export at their real size, and phone transfers stop timing out.
  • Video playback fixes. New Fit / Fill / Stretch content mode on video beats, previews no longer autoplay with audio in the beat editor, removing a video beat can no longer leave a ghost audio stream, and a macOS 26.5 launch crash tied to video scenes is fixed.
  • Keypad dial modes. Phone keypad beats now offer Typed mode, where any key an actor presses types the scripted number, and Free dial, which honors the literal digits; the outgoing-call beat inherits the dialed number automatically.
  • Games pass. Solitaire, Cascade, and Stack rebuilt to modern mobile-game layouts with original, license-safe art, and New Game no longer stacks decks.
  • Calendar browsing. The iPhone calendar surface now supports month navigation and swiping for free-roam scenes.

StageGlass Desktop Player — 1.1.0

The free-roam desktop grows up: production-art office packs, a living app suite, and a fleet remote.

  • Production-art free-roam packs. Desktops built from licensed production art with measured icon grids, taskbar hotspots, and chromeless art windows that stay pixel-aligned at any window size.
  • A real app suite for background actors. Browser with live history, search and rich authored web pages; Mail; Files with PDF viewing; Word-style documents with full-page scrolling; Firm Chat messenger with typed replies; Calendar; Contacts; Reminders; Notes; Settings.
  • Windows behave like windows. Resize with limits, minimize that animates into the taskbar, up to three windows per app, and per-seat startup layouts so a wall of machines never boots identical.
  • Desktop Wall fleet control (player side). Shuffle to a seeded state, capture and restore exact desktop states between takes, receive pushed packages over the network and auto-open them, and report live thumbnails to the operator.
  • On-set operator controls. Remote screensaver, keyboard/mouse input lock with a local emergency escape, blackout, brightness, and color grading including temperature.
  • Fill Entire Screen mode. Optional fullscreen that extends behind the MacBook camera housing so props read truly edge-to-edge on camera.
  • Quality of life. Ambient idle activity defers to real users, a visible warning when the remote port is taken by another app, and package swaps always start from a clean desktop.

item.title: Director 1.1.2
item.description:
StageGlass Director — 1.1.2

  • Instagram & Messages fidelity. A large sweep of iPhone-preview fixes: comment sheets, feed spacing, comment-overlay dismissal and threaded comments, scripted like handling, the reel share composer, profile back transitions, and message media — full-screen video viewer, play affordances on video attachments, media rendered above the thread chrome, and animated send insertion.
  • iOS 26 wallpaper picker. New gallery picker sectioned by collection, with the iOS 26 default wallpapers and a collection catalog; the device platform now flows into the picker.
  • Authoring. Shared ShowAuthorKit extracted for per-show author tools; Share Scene now warns on a missing asset instead of hard-blocking the export.
  • New show content. Gordie/Conviction iPhone scenes (101, 102, 104, 105) and continued Rachel Sings shoot-prep polish.
  • Free-roam desktop and Linux ops. Global Intel skin with ops map, intel feed, and recon app views plus scripted ops beats; free-roam home grid swipeability and overlay fixes.
  • Touch and interaction. dial_contact and Phone open-app now touch-drive on camera; fixed open-app-after-go-home actor taps and several home-grid hit-plane issues.

item.title: Director 1.1.1
item.description:
StageGlass Director — 1.1.1

A big rollup since 1.1.0 — the prop iPhone's Instagram is now built out for real, plus a scene-by-scene fidelity pass.

  • Instagram, fully built out. Live and climbing like counters, hashtag feed pages (like #singforacause), multi-slide carousel posts, comment streams that arrive over time, notification floods, a full Activity / Notifications screen, a real comment composer with nested replies, and opening another character's profile by searching their username.
  • Scene-audit fidelity fixes. A pass caught beats that dispatched fine but rendered the wrong thing on camera — placeholder filler comments over the wrong post, profile-grid stock-photo leaks, a carousel landing on the wrong slide, a like not landing on the right post, and future posts leaking into earlier scenes. All fixed.
  • Faithful iMessage photo attach. The real inline photo grid docked under the composer, with the two-step preview then send, matching iOS.
  • Cracked-screen effect. A thrown phone now shows authentic shattered glass with panel glitch — colored driver lines and a subtle flicker.
  • Messages fidelity. Per-message SMS green vs iMessage blue bubbles, structural inline replies, two-beat tapback reactions, and edit / unsend.
  • Scene Video + Stills export. Export any scene to a video plus per-beat stills for review.

StageGlass iOS Player — build 28 (1.3)

Rolls up everything above on the player side — the whole Instagram suite, the inline iMessage photo-attach flow, the cracked-screen effect, and the scene-audit fidelity fixes.

item.title: Director 1.1.0
item.description:
StageGlass Director — 1.1 (first auto-updating build)

This is the first Director build that updates itself — from here on, new versions arrive through Check for Updates instead of a hand-passed app. It also rolls up everything since 1.0.

Heads up — Video and Stills export is not working in this build. It currently runs only on a machine with Xcode and a simulator attached, so it won't export on a clean install. This is the top priority for the next update.

Working with scenes in bulk:

  • Batch actions across multiple scenes — select several scenes (same show) and Set Status, Move to Show, or export them together.
  • Batch scene-package export drops every selected scene into one folder.
  • Batch Export Video + Stills runs each scene in sequence from a single sheet *(subject to the export caveat above)*.
  • Multi-scene selection in the detail pane.

Messages authoring:

  • Message reactions — author a tap-back reaction with a target picker, and choose which character reacts.
  • Unsend and edit message beats.

Desktop Player (laptop props):

  • Driveable Live Preview you can step through with Shift+arrows / Esc.
  • Window-arrangement editor — drag windows to set where each app opens; open_app launches at that authored position.
  • Show Desktop beat and a prop-surface runtime for bespoke laptop screens.
  • Clip picker for prerecorded video-call footage (video_call_show_feed).

Scene overrides:

  • Per-scene Notes, Calendar, Music, and Reminders content toggles.
  • Format-aware scene sort; Season/Episode picker hidden for movie/other shows.

Under the hood:

  • Export bundles are now named after the scene (not its UUID), with clearer error messages and auto-discovery of iOSPlayer.app.
  • AI scene compiler moved to a testable layer with a drift-guard.
